name: Universal Housing Development Corporation
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor serving 9 Arkansas counties since 1971. Offers free/low-cost homebuying education, rental assistance, and home rehabilitation programs.
description_long:
Universal Housing Development Corporation (UHDC) is a nonprofit organization established in 1971 to provide housing-related services across central Arkansas. As a HUD-approved Housing Counseling agency and chartered member of NeighborWorks® America, UHDC operates from a mission of "Helping People, Building Dreams" and serves Conway, Johnson, Franklin, Logan, Perry, Polk, Pope, Scott, and Yell County residents. The organization maintains three office locations across the service area to maximize accessibility.

UHDC's core offerings include homebuyer education courses, housing counseling, self-help home construction programs, rental assistance administration, and home rehabilitation services. Their homebuyer courses have graduated 2,649 participants, indicating significant reach in financial literacy and housing preparation. They facilitate self-help home construction (1,377 homes built), manage substantial rental assistance programs (40,543 housed), and have rehabilitated 8,339 homes through their programs. The organization also coordinates volunteer opportunities and accepts donations to support operations.

What distinguishes UHDC is their deep regional roots and comprehensive service model spanning four decades. Their HUD-approval and NeighborWorks® charter membership signal compliance with federal standards for housing counseling. The breadth of their impact—from ownership preparation to rental support to physical home improvements—addresses multiple barriers to stable housing rather than isolated financial products. Their multi-county presence with dedicated local offices demonstrates commitment to geographic accessibility.

UHDC is appropriate for individuals seeking legitimate, nonprofit housing counseling without sales pressure or predatory product offerings. However, the website provides limited detail about specific eligibility requirements, program costs, wait times, or detailed service descriptions. Their scope appears primarily educational and rehabilitative rather than emergency financial relief. Prospective clients should contact their local office directly for program-specific information and availability.
pros: HUD-approved Housing Counseling agency—verified federal certification for legitimate services
Chartered NeighborWorks® America member—operates under established nonprofit standards
45+ year track record since 1971—demonstrates sustainability and community trust
Multi-county service area (9 counties) with 3 physical office locations—regional accessibility
Comprehensive housing services model—addresses homebuying, rental assistance, and home rehabilitation
Substantial documented community impact—2,649 homebuyer graduates, 40,543 housed through rental assistance
No indication of fees for counseling—typical of HUD-approved nonprofits offering free guidance
cons: Website lacks specific pricing, eligibility criteria, and program application details—minimal transparency about access
Limited service area restricted to 9 Arkansas counties—not available nationally or to most U.S. consumers
No online registration or intake system visible—requires phone/in-person contact for service inquiries
Website content sparse on detailed program descriptions—unclear which services are free vs. low-cost
No mention of emergency financial assistance—geared toward long-term housing solutions, not immediate cash needs
best_for: First-time homebuyers in central Arkansas seeking free or low-cost purchase education and pre-approval counseling
Low-income Arkansas residents needing rental assistance programs and landlord navigation support
Homeowners in UHDC's 9-county service area seeking guidance on home rehabilitation and improvement options
diagnosis: UHDC is best for Arkansas residents seeking legitimate, federally-approved nonprofit housing counseling and education without pressure to purchase financial products. Primary caveat: service is geographically limited to 9 central Arkansas counties and the website offers minimal detail on specific eligibility, costs, and application processes—direct contact required.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
